title: Thrust vectoring for fluid borne vehicles
abstract: Example implementations provide fluid-borne vehicles comprising a body and a plurality of thrust vectoring modules, each thrust vectoring module comprising a set of thrust producing means, wherein a first thrust producing means, mounted on a first mounting bar having a first mounting bar axis, is rotatable about the mounting bar axis and the mounting bar axis is rotatable about an arm having an arm axis that is nonparallel to the mounting bar axis; and a second thrust producing means, mounted on a second mounting bar having a second mounting bar axis, is rotatable about the second mounting bar axis and the second mounting bar axis is rotatable about the arm axis that is nonparallel to the second mounting bar axis.
claims: Claims 1. A fluid-borne vehicle comprising a body and a plurality of thrust vectoring modules, each thrust vectoring module comprising a set of thrust producing means, wherein a first thrust producing means, mounted on a first mounting bar having a first mounting bar axis, is rotatable about the mounting bar axis and the mounting bar axis is rotatable about an arm having an arm axis that is nonparallel to the mounting bar axis; and a second thrust producing means, mounted on a second mounting bar having a second mounting bar axis, is rotatable about the second mounting bar axis and the second mounting bar axis is rotatable about the arm axis that is nonparallel to the second mounting bar axis. 2. The vehicle of claim 1 , wherein: a first thrust vectoring module of the plurality of thrust vectoring modules comprises the first thrust producing means mounted on the first mounting bar, and a second thrust vectoring module of the plurality thrust vectoring modules comprises the second thrust producing means mounted on the second mounting bar. 3. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which the first and second mounting bars are coupled to the same arm. 4. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which the first mounting bar axis is rotatable about the arm axis by at least one of differential thrusts or differential moments associated with a plurality of thrust producing means of the respective first thrust vectoring module to produce a respective moment that causes the first mounting bar axis to rotate about the arm axis. 5. The vehicle of any preceding claim wherein the second mounting bar axis is rotatable about the arm axis by at least one of differential thrusts or differential moments associated with a plurality of thrust producing means of the respective second thrust vectoring module to produce a respective moment that causes the second mounting bar axis to rotate about the arm axis. 6. The vehicle of any preceding claim comprising at least one actuator arranged to rotate at least the first thrust producing means about the first axis. 7. The vehicle of claim 6 in which the at least one actuator arranged to rotate the at least the first thrust producing means about the first axis is operable to vary the orientation of an axis of rotation of at least the first thrust producing means. 8. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which each or a thrust vectoring module comprises at least two thrust producing means. 9. The vehicle of claim 8 in which the first thrust producing means and a further thrust producing means are distally disposed on the first mounting bar either side of the arm axis. 10. The vehicle of either of claims 8 and 9 in which the second thrust producing means and a still further thrust producing means are distally disposed on the second mounting bar either side of the arm axis. 1 1 . The vehicle of any preceding claim in which at least one thrust vectoring module of the plurality of thrust vectoring modules, or at least one thrust producing means, is freely rotatable about the at least one of the first mounting bar axis or the second mounting bar axis. 12. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which the body is an elongate body having a longitudinal axis wherein at least one of the first mounting bar axis or the second mounting bar axis is parallel to the longitudinal axis, and the arm axis is orthogonal to the longitudinal axis. 13. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which the plurality of thrust vectoring modules comprises at least two thrust vectoring modules disposed at respective ends of the body. 14. The vehicle of claim 13 in which the plurality of thrust vectoring modules comprises at least four thrust vectoring modules with at least two pairs of thrust vectoring modules being disposed at respective ends of the body. 15. The vehicle of any preceding claim comprising a controller to control: at least one of the thrust or orientation of one or more than one of the plurality of thrust producing means, one or more than one thrust vectoring module, or both. 16. The vehicle of claim 15 in which the controller is arranged to orientate a thrust producing means relative to a further thrust producing means to reduce the influence of thrust from said thrust producing means on said further thrust producing means. 17. A thrust vectoring assembly to vector thrust, the assembly comprising: at least two rotors, a rotor mount bar, having a mount axis, bearing the at least two rotors; an arm having an arm axis that is nonparallel to the mount axis; wherein the rotor mount bar is rotatable about the mount axis and is rotatable about the arm axis. 18. The assembly of claim 17 comprising an actuator to rotate the rotor mount bar about the mount axis. 19. The assembly of either of claim 17 and 18 in which the mount axis is disposed orthogonally relative to the arm axis. 20. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 19 in which the at least two rotors are disposed at opposite ends of the rotor mount bar. 21 . The assembly of any of claims 17 to 20 in which the rotor mount bar is rotatably connected, at a centre point of the rotor mount bar, to the arm. 22. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 21 in which the rotor mount bar is rotatably connected, at a centre point of the rotor mount bar, to the arm to couple at least one of moments and forces associated with the at least two rotors to the arm. 23. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 22 in which the rotor mount bar bearing the at least two rotors is rotatable through a predetermined angle. 24. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 23 in which the at least two rotors are located on the rotor mount bar at positions to provide clearance between the arm and the at least two rotors. 25. A thrust vectored vehicle comprising a body and at least one thrust vector assembly of any of claims 17 to 24 connected to the body. 26. The vehicle of claim 25 in which the at least one thrust vector assembly comprising a plurality of thrust vector assemblies connected to the body. 27. A thrust vectored vehicle comprising: a vehicle body comprising an arm, having an arm axis, the arm carrying a rotor bearing mount rotatable about a mount axis, nonparallel to the arm axis, by an actuator; the rotor bearing mount being rotatable about the arm axis. 28. The fluid-borne vehicle of any preceding claim, wherein the first mounting bar axis is rotatable about the arm axis by individually varying the thrust produced by a plurality of thrust producing means in order to produce a moment that causes the first mounting bar axis to rotate about the second axis. 29. A fluid-borne vehicle comprising a thrust vectoring module, wherein each thrust vectoring module comprises a plurality of thrust producing means, wherein a first plurality of thrust producing means is rotatable about a first axis and the first axis is rotatable about a second axis, which is not substantially parallel to the first axis; and a second plurality of thrust producing means is rotatable about a third axis and the third axis is rotatable about the second axis, which is not substantially parallel to the third axis. 30. The fluid-borne vehicle of claim 29, wherein the first axis is rotatable about the second axis by individually varying the thrust produced by a plurality of thrust producing means in order to produce a moment that causes the first axis to rotate about the second axis. 31 . The fluid-borne vehicle of claim 30, wherein the first axis is rotatable about the second axis by an actuator. 32. The vehicle of any of preceding claim, wherein the first mounting bar axis and the second mounting bar axis are in fixed relation to each other. 33. The vehicle of any preceding claim, wherein the vehicle is adapted to carry a sensor. 34. The vehicle of any preceding claim, wherein the vehicle is adapted to launch a projectile or fire a beam of directed energy. 35. A fluid-borne vehicle as in any of the preceding claims, wherein in a first configuration of the vehicle, the exhaust from a thrust producing means of the first thrust vectoring module substantially impinges on a thrust producing means of the second thrust vectoring module when the vehicle body is in a certain orientation and the first thrust vectoring module is generating a certain net force and a certain net moment on the vehicle; in a second configuration of the vehicle, the exhaust from no thrust producing means of the first thrust vectoring module substantially impinges on any thrust producing means of the second thrust vectoring module when the vehicle body is substantially in the same orientation and the first thrust vectoring module is producing substantially the same net force and net moment on the vehicle; and the vehicle is adapted to avoid the first configuration in preference to the second. 36. The vehicle of any preceding claim in which each thrust producing means has a respective axis of rotation that intersects an axis of a respective mounting bar.
